findings.title: Signed minimum slice limits crash the Rust CLI
findings.claim: The documented signed-i64 limit value -9223372036854775808 is accepted by clap but Rust negates it directly, causing an overflow panic instead of Python-compatible tail truncation.
findings.reproduction: Ran both CLIs with `.goalloop-support/triage/F8/facts.json`, `F8/slices.yml`, and `--by-slice=-9223372036854775808`. Python exited 0 with `{"slices":[],"summary":{"matching_pct":100.0,"matching_time_ns":7,"total_time_ns":7}}`. Rust exited 101 with `panicked at crates/clankerprof-core/src/slices.rs:334:39: attempt to negate with overflow`.

findings.title: Rust drops valid scope buckets above i64::MAX
findings.claim: Scope rendering reads bucket totals only through `as_i64()`, so a valid aggregate in the documented u64 range is treated as zero and removed.
findings.reproduction: Mutated the F8 facts fixture to contain two otherwise-identical samples valued 9223372036854775807, with summary total 18446744073709551614, then ran `scopes` with `F8/scopes_valid.yml`. Python exited 0 with a bucket whose `time_ns` was 18446744073709551614 and whose AppWork category contained both samples. Rust exited 0 with `{"total_time_ns":18446744073709551614,"buckets":[]}`. This total is the spec's explicit valid example.

findings.title: Occurrence scopes invalidate the aggregate-bound proof and panic Rust
findings.claim: The aggregate bound assumes every projection is a subset sum, but default scope semantics multiply a sample by repeated scope occurrences. Valid bounded facts can therefore exceed u64 in derived data; Python emits an out-of-contract integer and Rust panics while serializing it.
findings.reproduction: Used one valid sample valued 9223372036854775807 with stack indexes `[0,1,1,1]`, where frame 1 is scope T repeated three times, and ran the default occurrence-mode scope config. Python exited 0 with `{"total_time_ns":27670116110564327421,"pct_of_profile":300.0,"samples":3,"leaf_cpu":27670116110564327421}`. Rust exited 101 at `scopes.rs:704` with `called Result::unwrap() on an Err value: Error("number out of range")`.

findings.title: Rust compare rejects valid u64 report totals
findings.claim: Rust's compare reader requires `summary.total_time_ns` to fit i64 even though projection reports may validly contain totals through u64::MAX.
findings.reproduction: Compared identical reports `{"tool":"clankerprof_slices","summary":{"total_time_ns":18446744073709551614},"slices":[{"name":"A","pct":50}]}`. Python exited 0 and returned exact `before_total_ns` and `after_total_ns` values of 18446744073709551614. Rust exited 2 with `{"error":"Report summary field 'total_time_ns' must be an integer.","ok":false}`.

findings.title: Compare still fails open when a row's pct field is missing
findings.claim: The round-one strictness fix leaves absent per-row numeric fields defaulting to zero. A corrupt candidate report is accepted as an improvement, allowing the regression gate to exit successfully.
findings.reproduction: Compared before `{"tool":"clankerprof_slices","summary":{"total_time_ns":100},"slices":[{"name":"hot","pct":10}]}` with after `{"tool":"clankerprof_slices","summary":{"total_time_ns":100},"slices":[{"name":"hot"}]}`. Both CLIs exited 0 and emitted `{"has_regression":false,"slices":[{"after_pct":0.0,"before_pct":10.0,"delta_abs":-10.0,"delta_rel":-100.0,"name":"hot","status":"improvement"}]}` instead of rejecting the malformed report.

findings.title: Rust regex handling is incompatible and can fail open
findings.claim: The documented configuration language uses Python regular expressions, including lookahead, but Rust's regex engine cannot load them. Outside rule-pack loading, compilation failure is silently converted to no-match, so even malformed explicit regexes produce valid-looking reports.
findings.reproduction: With F8 facts and a runtime pack containing `semantic_rules: [{category: Lookahead, name_patterns: ["^(?=.*Leaf)Leaf$"]}]`, Python exited 0 and attributed all 7 ns to Lookahead; Rust exited 2 with `Invalid runtime rule name pattern "^(?=.*Leaf)Leaf$".` The repository documentation itself shows a `(?=.*View)` lookahead. Separately, target config `{"T":{"Bad":"regex:["}}` made Python exit 2 with `Invalid regex pattern '['`, while Rust exited 0 and attributed 100% to Other.

findings.title: Signed sample costs disappear from scope rollups
findings.claim: Both renderers discard categories, buckets, and owners whose aggregate is non-positive. Because facts explicitly allow signed i64 sample values, mixed-sign reports cease to be additive and can report more bucket time than total scope time.
findings.reproduction: Ran a valid two-sample facts document under scope T: Pos=10 and Neg=-5, with cost kinds matching each leaf. Both CLIs exited 0 with scope `total_time_ns:5` and `samples:2`, but emitted only Pos: `{"buckets":[{"time_ns":10,"pct":200.0,"samples":1,"categories":[{"name":"Pos","time_ns":10}]}]}`. Neg was absent, so rollup rows summed to 10 rather than the required total 5.

findings.title: Facts-v2 import silently defaults required sample fields
findings.claim: Both importers treat missing `values`, `location_ids`, and `stack` keys as empty arrays, contradicting the facts-v2 field contract and the CLI guarantee that missing facts keys are validation errors.
findings.reproduction: Deleted only `samples[0].values` from the otherwise-valid F8 facts fixture and changed its summary total to 0. With target config `{"T":{"App":"path:/app"}}`, both CLIs exited 0 and emitted `{"total_time_ns":0,"categories":[{"name":"App","time_ns":0,"samples":1}]}`. Neither emitted the required exit-2 error.

findings.title: Published byte parity fails on valid JSON lexical cases
findings.claim: Non-facts JSON artifacts are not byte-identical: Python ASCII-escapes Unicode while Rust emits UTF-8, and their float serializers use different exponent spelling.
findings.reproduction: For a valid target fact containing `/café/x`, Python emitted `"/caf\u00e9/x"` while Rust emitted `"/café/x"`; both exited 0. For valid target categories valued 1 and 99999999, Python emitted the small percentage as `1e-06`, while Rust emitted `1e-6`; all other observed fields matched. These directly contradict the claimed byte-level artifact comparison.

findings.title: Rust scope config validation silently substitutes defaults
findings.claim: Rust accepts malformed predicate tables and non-string count modes that Python rejects, then produces ordinary-looking scope artifacts using no-match or occurrence defaults.
findings.reproduction:
With F8 facts, config `cost_kind:
  Empty: {}
scope:
  - function: T` made Python exit 2 with `cost_kind Empty predicate table cannot be empty`; Rust exited 0 with all 7 ns under Other. With `count: 1`, Python exited 2 with `scope.count must be occurrence or once_per_sample`, while Rust exited 0 with scope T totaling 7 ns and one sample.

findings.title: An optional library-regex capture escapes the Python error boundary
findings.claim: A valid regex whose first capture group does not participate returns None in Python and causes an uncaught AttributeError. Rust instead completes with a different extraction result.
findings.reproduction: Used a valid custom rule pack with `library_path_patterns: ["regex:/gems/(foo)?bar/"]`, a default slice, and one fact at `/gems/bar/file.rb`. Python exited 1 with a traceback ending `patterns.py:186: AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'strip'`. Rust exited 0 and emitted an unattributed library named `gems/bar`.

findings.title: Rust still omits public runtime-analysis flags
findings.claim: Additional Python runtime flag surfaces remain absent from Rust despite the specification claiming all target runtime flags and full scope decomposition parity.
findings.reproduction: `clankerprof scopes --facts F8/facts.json --config F8/scopes_valid.yml --fold-runtime-internals` exited 0 with a scope artifact; the equivalent Rust command exited 2 with `unexpected argument '--fold-runtime-internals'`. Likewise Python targets accepted `--verbose-runtime-internals` and emitted an artifact, while Rust exited 2 with `unexpected argument '--verbose-runtime-internals'`. Rust scopes also has no `--no-enhanced` wiring.

findings.title: Rust reorders scope cost-kind definitions and changes first-match attribution
findings.claim: Rust converts YAML/TOML scope mappings through a sorted serde_json map, losing declaration order even though configured cost kinds are evaluated first-match-wins.
findings.reproduction:
Ran valid config `cost_kind:
  ZFirst: "path:/srv/app"
  ASecond: "path:/srv/app"
scope:
  - function: T` against F8 facts. Both definitions match the leaf. Python exited 0 and selected `ZFirst`, the declared first cost kind; Rust exited 0 and selected alphabetically earlier `ASecond`.

findings.title: Multi-parent target CSV order differs between Python and Rust
findings.claim: Rust stores target results in a BTreeMap while Python preserves first-seen parent order, so claimed byte parity for CSV, simple-CSV, and text outputs fails whenever parent encounter order is not alphabetical.
findings.reproduction: Used one valid sample with stack `Leaf -> ZTarget -> ATarget`, config `{"ZTarget":{},"ATarget":{}}`, and `--format simple-csv`. Python rows were `ZTarget,Other,...` then `ATarget,Other,...`; Rust rows were `ATarget,Other,...` then `ZTarget,Other,...`. Both exited 0.

summary: Found 14 reproduced defects, including two valid-input Rust panics and a residual fail-open compare gate. `./bin/dev check` passed, including its Rust test lane. A separate `cargo test --workspace` invocation could not start in the mandated read-only sandbox because Cargo was denied access to `target/debug/.cargo-build-lock`; retrying with `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp` was also denied, so that environmental failure is not listed as a repository finding.
